page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton processor utilizing the ARM64 architecture. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance features dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 design. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance bundles a 5000 GB NVMe SSD, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i8ge.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong read-only large block memory bandwidth of 189.45 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also register in the top 25% of tested systems. Most general compute metrics, including stress-ng single-core and multi-core performance, Geekbench software compilation, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average range. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic encryption tests and medium-model LLM prompt processing.

The combination of 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 64.0 GB of memory on an 8-core ARM64 architecture offers high resource density. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads by reducing the need for external network-attached storage. The instance is highly suited for local database hosting, high-throughput storage operations, memory-cached applications, and text-processing pipelines that benefit from local NVMe speed and high memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64.0 GB of memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes a 5000 GB local NVMe SSD alongside an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 189.45 GB/sec and strong text processing capabilities. While general compute and database operations perform in the average range, cryptographic encryption and certain LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance. The high local storage capacity and memory-to-core ratio make this instance a cost-efficient choice for database hosting, caching layers, and data-intensive storage workloads without the overhead of external storage arrays.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 5000 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 5000 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and dense memory allocation for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 64.0 GB of memory and 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier memory performance and strong large-block read bandwidth
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database hosting, caching, and storage-heavy workloads
